commands.review_summary:
The branch changes shared Google provider message conversion to accumulate consecutive tool responses for one model turn before flushing Gemini <3 image-result user turns, and adds a focused regression test.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +78. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main's last-content heuristic can split a parallel Gemini <3 tool-result run when an image turn is inserted between responses, and the added test exercises that exact payload shape. I did not run local tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A human maintainer or the contributor needs to provide or accept external API proof; there is no narrow code repair for automation to perform on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(google): keep parallel Gemini tool responses in the turn after the model
target.body:
## Summary

On Gemini < 3 vision models (gemini-2.5-*), a single assistant turn with parallel tool calls where a non-last result returns an image corrupts the request so the next call returns 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT. Because the malformed turn is persisted in session history, every subsequent turn re-serializes it and re-400s, so the session is stuck until the user compacts or starts over.

Root cause is in `convertMessages` (`src/llm/providers/google-shared.ts`). Gemini's contract: the user turn immediately following a model turn with N functionCall parts must carry all N matching functionResponse parts, or the API returns 400 (confirmed against gemini-cli #16135 and litellm #26755). For Gemini < 3 (no multimodal functionResponse) an image-bearing tool result emits a separate "Tool result image:" user turn. The old merge heuristic only inspected `contents[contents.length - 1]`: when that separate image turn lands between two parallel function responses, the heuristic sees the image turn (not a functionResponse turn) and starts a fresh user turn for the second response. The turn right after the model then holds only 1 of 2 responses, so the count mismatches and Gemini 400s. It is order-dependent: if the image-bearing result is last it merges fine, which is why it slipped through.

Produced `contents` before the fix: `[user, model(fc1,fc2), user(fr1), user(image), user(fr2)]`, with fr2 stranded behind the image turn.

## Fix

Replace the fragile `contents[last]` heuristic with a run-scoped accumulator. All function responses for one model turn merge into the single user turn right after it; Gemini < 3 image turns are deferred and flushed at the end of the tool-result run so they trail the merged response turn. After the fix: `[user, model(fc1,fc2), user(fr1,fr2), user(image)]`, both responses in the immediately following turn.

Both Gemini surfaces, `google.ts` (Gemini API) and `google-vertex.ts` (Cloud Code Assist / Vertex), route through this single `convertMessages`, so the one change covers both. This is a latent bug in the Gemini-<3 separate-image-turn path (landed around 2026-05-31), not a fresh regression.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** On gemini-2.5-* a parallel tool-call turn whose non-last result returns an image made the provider serialize a request whose user turn right after the model carried fewer functionResponse parts than the model issued functionCall parts. Gemini rejects that with 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT, and because the malformed turn is persisted every later turn in the session re-sends it and re-400s (stuck session).

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at this PR head. Drove the real Google provider request-assembly path (`convertMessages` in `src/llm/providers/google-shared.ts`, the function that builds the `contents[]` body the provider POSTs to the Gemini generateContent endpoint) with `node --import tsx` for model `gemini-2.5-flash`, against both the patched tree and the production file restored to origin/main.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx gemini-parallel-proof.ts`, a parallel screenshot + weather tool turn where the non-last result is an image, printing the serialized `contents[]` the provider would POST and comparing the model functionCall count to the functionResponse count in the user turn immediately after the model.

**Evidence after fix:** live console output from the patched tree:

```
Request contents[] that the Google provider POSTs to generativeContent:
  [0] role=user: text("Screenshot the page and check the weather.")
  [1] role=model: functionCall(screenshot), functionCall(weather)
  [2] role=user: functionResponse(screenshot), functionResponse(weather)
  [3] role=user: text("Tool result image:"), inlineData(image)

model turn issued 2 functionCall parts
user turn immediately after the model carries 2 functionResponse parts
RESULT: counts match (2=2) -> Gemini accepts the request
```

Same command with only the production file restored to origin/main, showing the defect:

```
Request contents[] that the Google provider POSTs to generativeContent:
  [0] role=user: text("Screenshot the page and check the weather.")
  [1] role=model: functionCall(screenshot), functionCall(weather)
  [2] role=user: functionResponse(screenshot)
  [3] role=user: text("Tool result image:"), inlineData(image)
  [4] role=user: functionResponse(weather)

model turn issued 2 functionCall parts
user turn immediately after the model carries 1 functionResponse parts
RESULT: count MISMATCH (2!=1) -> Gemini rejects with 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT (stuck session)
```

**Observed result after fix:** the serialized request now carries both functionResponse parts in the user turn immediately after the model (counts match, 2 = 2), with the image content trailing in its own user turn, so Gemini accepts the request. Before the patch that same turn carried only 1 of 2 responses (2 != 1), the second stranded behind the image turn, which is the 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT and the stuck session.

**What was not tested:** no live Gemini 2.5 HTTP round-trip from this machine (no Gemini credentials available here). The proof above is the exact request payload the provider serializes and would POST; the 400 contract for a functionResponse-count mismatch is confirmed against gemini-cli #16135 and litellm #26755.

## Verification

Regression test `src/llm/providers/google-shared.parallel-image-repro.test.ts` asserts the user turn after the model carries both responses and nothing is stranded. It is red on origin/main (`expected 1 to be 2`) and green with the fix. The existing google-shared convert and conversion suites stay green (14 cases), and the changed files pass tsgo (`tsgo:core:test`) and oxlint.
